The previous steps have exported but maybe not captured the needed constants. The frontier suggests expand_file [F1] with priority 112. That likely will expand more symbols. So next action could be to run session.expand_file with target_selector maybe "@crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs" again but with depth or limit to capture more symbols. Or perhaps use session.hydrate_source to hydrate the source for artifacts.rs to capture schema version constants. The earlier history includes "Hydrating source for artifacts.rs to capture schema version constants" with session.hydrate_source@v1. That might be what we need: hydrate source to capture constants. So we could call session.hydrate_source with target_selector referencing the file.

Thus the next tool call likely is session.hydrate_source with target_selector "crates/graphbench-core/src/artifacts.rs". That would hydrate source with padding maybe. The payload for session.hydrate_source includes target selector and optional padding and hydration budgets. So we can call it.
